hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Try to perform a prefix sum on $$$a$$$.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Consider what the upper bound of the answer is.

solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
2222D - Permutation Construction
First, let $$$b_i=\sum_{j=1}^{i-1} a_j$$$. Then the value of the inversion $$$(i,j)$$$ can be expressed as $$$b_j-b_i$$$.
Consider maximizing the beauty of the permutation $$$p$$$. The ideal situation is that for all $$$(i,j)$$$ where $$$b_j \gt b_i$$$ and $$$i \lt j$$$, $$$p_i \gt p_j$$$ is satisfied, and for all $$$(i,j)$$$ where $$$b_j \lt b_i$$$ and $$$i \lt j$$$, $$$p_i \lt p_j$$$ is satisfied.
We find that even when $$$i \lt j$$$ is not guaranteed, the magnitude relationship between $$$p_i$$$ and $$$p_j$$$ is the opposite of the magnitude relationship between $$$b_i$$$ and $$$b_j$$$. Therefore, we can sort all $$$i$$$ in descending order by $$$b_i$$$ and set $$$p_i=x$$$ for the $$$x$$$-th $$$i$$$, which exactly meets all the above requirements.
This entire process uses only sorting and has a time complexity of $$$O(n\log n)$$$.
